     The Numbers Business Report

She’s the Man (2006)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $33,889,159Details
International Box Office $21,390,449
Worldwide Box Office $55,279,608
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $35,273,082
Total $35,273,082
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Viola Johnson is in a real jam. Complications threaten her scheme to pose as her twin brother, Sebastian, and take his place at a new boarding school. She falls in love with her handsome roommate, Duke, who loves beautiful Olivia, who has fallen for Sebastian! As if that were not enough, Viola's twin returns from London ahead of schedule but has no idea that his sister has already replaced him on campus.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$10,730,372 (31.7% of total gross)
Legs:3.16 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:61.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,623 opening theaters/2,631 max. theaters, 4.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $58,517,001

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 17th, 2006 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
Video Release: July 18th, 2006 by Dreamworks Video
July 24th, 2006 by Entertainment in Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some sexual material
Running Time: 105 minutes
Plot point: Cross-Dressing, Pretending to be your Twin, Romance, Soccer, Twins
Social setting: Boarding School
Source material: Modern Adaptation
Subgenre: Romantic Comedy
Source:Based on Play
Genre:Romantic Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Donners’ Company
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
